titleStmt.title: Letter from John R Moorhead to the Chief Secretary's Office, 12 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John R Moorhead

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John R Moorhead to the Chief Secretary's Office regarding a claim for reimbursement of expenses by William Logan who was called as a witness in the court case The King against McBride or Watters.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Counties of Antrim & Belfast Crown Solicitor's Office.
30 Rosemary Street.
Belfast. 12th. August 1916 SIR/ Referring to the attached claim of William Logan, I beg to
report that the claimant attended as a witness in the case of The
King against McBride or Watters who was charged with Bigamy.
The claimant was returned to me as having at tended four days and
that he had travelled 132 miles on the railway. I allowed him at
the rate of 6/- per day for loss of time:- £1. 4. 0. and 13/- for
travelling expenses making a total of £1. 17. 0., and I consider he
has been paid fairly. For instance he says he had to travel each
day to xxxx Belfast, and for this purpose must have had a Subscription
ticket for the Railway, and assuming this to be so, the 13/- allowed
by me for rail fare would more than compensate the claimant for car
fare to the Railway. I am, Sir,
Your obedt. servant John R Moorhead Send usual answer
declining to interfere
I.J.T.
14.8.16
